---
title: "Management Zone Delineation Report"
subtitle: "Fuzzy C-Means with PCA dimensionality reduction and ordinary kriging"
author: "`{r} params$author`"
date: today
date-format: "long"
lang: en
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show R code"
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 5
    fig-dpi: 150
    theme: cosmo
    df-print: kable
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    fig-width: 6.5
    fig-height: 4
    df-print: kable
    keep-tex: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: false
params:
  outputs_dir: "outputs"
  data_dir: "data"
  # Optional explicit input paths. When empty (the default), the report falls
  # back to the demo filenames inside data_dir — this is the batch-script path.
  # The Shiny app passes the user's uploaded files here so the report reflects
  # the actual inputs instead of the bundled example.
  boundary_path: ""
  raster_path: ""
  points_path: ""
  # Soil variables to analyze. Empty => use the built-in default set. Pass a
  # comma-separated string (e.g. "BD,CEC,SOC") to match the caller's columns.
  soil_vars: ""
  author: "MZ Analysis"
  study_area_name: "Study Area"
  k_range: [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
  fuzziness: 2
  pca_threshold: 0.80
  transition_threshold: 0.60
  interpolation_method: "kriging"
  seed: 42
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(terra)
  library(sf)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(knitr)
})

# ---- Resolve paths relative to the project root ----------------------------
# The .qmd lives in `report/`; the project root is its parent. We resolve
# `params$outputs_dir` and `params$data_dir` against that root so that
# `quarto render report/mz_report.qmd` works regardless of the caller's CWD.

qmd_dir <- normalizePath(dirname(knitr::current_input()), mustWork = TRUE)
project_root <- normalizePath(file.path(qmd_dir, ".."), mustWork = TRUE)

resolve_from_root <- function(p) {
  if (is.null(p) || !nzchar(p)) return(p)
  if (file.exists(p)) return(normalizePath(p, mustWork = TRUE))
  candidate <- file.path(project_root, p)
  if (file.exists(candidate)) return(normalizePath(candidate, mustWork = TRUE))
  p
}

OUT <- resolve_from_root(params$outputs_dir)
DATA <- resolve_from_root(params$data_dir)

# Soil variables to analyze. If the caller passed a comma-separated list
# (e.g. the Shiny app, reflecting the user's column selection), use it;
# otherwise fall back to the batch script's default variable set.
SOIL_VARS <- if (!is.null(params$soil_vars) && nzchar(params$soil_vars)) {
  trimws(strsplit(params$soil_vars, ",")[[1]])
} else {
  c("BD", "CEC", "Fragm", "Sand", "Silt", "Clay",
    "N", "OCD", "pH", "SOC")
}

# ---- File existence check --------------------------------------------------
# `mz_transition.tif` and `mz_point_assignments.csv` are *optional*. The Shiny
# app does not always write the transition mask (it has no UI control for the
# transition threshold) and may not persist point assignments to disk. Missing
# optional files show a small notice in the report rather than aborting render.
required <- c(
  file.path(OUT, "mz_validation.csv"),
  file.path(OUT, "mz_zone_stats.csv"),
  file.path(OUT, "mz_anova.csv"),
  file.path(OUT, "mz_zone_map.tif")
)
optional <- c(
  file.path(OUT, "mz_point_assignments.csv"),
  file.path(OUT, "mz_transition.tif")
)
missing <- required[!file.exists(required)]
missing_optional <- optional[!file.exists(optional)]
if (length(missing) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required outputs: ",
       paste(missing, collapse = ", "),
       "\nProject root resolved to: ", project_root, "\n",
       "Run `delineation_management_zones.R` first.")
}
```

# Executive summary

```{r exec-summary-load}
validation <- read.csv(file.path(OUT, "mz_validation.csv"))

# The Shiny app's export handler writes mz_validation.csv WITHOUT the
# `*_rank` columns (it just dumps rv$validation_df to disk), while the
# batch script adds XB_rank / FPI_rank / NCE_rank / consensus_rank
# before writing. Compute them here if missing so the report is
# robust to either source.
if (!"consensus_rank" %in% names(validation)) {
  for (idx in c("XB", "FPI", "NCE")) {
    rk_col <- paste0(idx, "_rank")
    if (!rk_col %in% names(validation)) {
      validation[[rk_col]] <- rank(validation[[idx]], ties.method = "min")
    }
  }
  validation$consensus_rank <- validation$XB_rank +
    validation$FPI_rank + validation$NCE_rank
  cat("Note: mz_validation.csv had no rank columns; computed on the fly.\n")
}

opt_k <- validation$k[which.min(validation$consensus_rank)]
n_soil_vars <- length(SOIL_VARS)

# Optional: point assignments
pa_path <- file.path(OUT, "mz_point_assignments.csv")
has_pa <- file.exists(pa_path)
point_assignments <- if (has_pa) read.csv(pa_path) else NULL
n_points <- if (has_pa) nrow(point_assignments) else NA_integer_

# Optional: zone map. The hard zone raster is required; the membership
# rasters (mz_membership_z*.tif) are needed for the uncertainty map.
zone_map <- rast(file.path(OUT, "mz_zone_map.tif"))
zone_vals <- values(zone_map, mat = FALSE)
n_cells <- ncell(zone_map)
valid_cells <- sum(!is.na(zone_vals))
n_zones <- length(unique(zone_vals[!is.na(zone_vals)]))

# Optional: transition mask
trans_path <- file.path(OUT, "mz_transition.tif")
has_transition <- file.exists(trans_path)
pct_transition <- if (has_transition) {
  transition <- rast(trans_path)
  round(100 * mean(values(transition, mat = FALSE) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
} else {
  NA_real_
}
```

This report summarizes the delineation of **`r n_zones` management zones** over a study area
(`r params$study_area_name`) covering **`r format(valid_cells, big.mark = ",")` valid raster cells**
(out of `r format(n_cells, big.mark = ",")` total) at the resolution of the input soil-property
raster. Zone counts were evaluated over `r paste(params$k_range, collapse = ", ")`, and the
optimal value **k = `r opt_k`** was selected by the consensus of the Xie-Beni (XB), Fuzzy
Performance Index (FPI), and Normalized Classification Entropy (NCE) indices.

The analysis used `r if (has_pa) paste0("**", n_points, "** observation points and ") else ""`**`r n_soil_vars` soil properties**
(`r paste(SOIL_VARS, collapse = ", ")`). Membership surfaces were interpolated by
**`r params$interpolation_method`**; `r if (has_transition) paste0(pct_transition, "% of the study area falls in a transition zone (maximum membership below ", params$transition_threshold, ").") else "no transition mask was written to disk; the per-cell uncertainty (entropy) is reported in section 8."`

# 1. Inputs

```{r inputs-load}
# Resolve each input path: use the explicit param when the caller supplied a
# readable file (e.g. the Shiny app with the user's upload); otherwise fall
# back to the demo filename inside DATA (the batch-script path). This keeps
# every downstream section (boundary figure, pre-processing mask, PCA, ANOVA,
# entropy mask) consistent with whichever inputs actually produced outputs/.
resolve_input <- function(param_val, demo_name) {
  if (!is.null(param_val) && nzchar(param_val) && file.exists(param_val)) {
    return(normalizePath(param_val, mustWork = TRUE))
  }
  file.path(DATA, demo_name)
}

boundary_path <- resolve_input(params$boundary_path, "agro_geo.gpkg")
raster_path   <- resolve_input(params$raster_path,   "soil_predictions.tif")
points_path   <- resolve_input(params$points_path,   "soilgrids_data.csv")

boundary <- if (file.exists(boundary_path)) st_read(boundary_path, quiet = TRUE) else NULL
soil_stack <- if (file.exists(raster_path)) rast(raster_path) else NULL
points_raw <- if (file.exists(points_path)) read.csv(points_path) else NULL

# Raster CRS, reused by the §1.1 narrative and by the pre-processing chunk
# (boundary reprojection). Computed here so the inline prose below can show it.
raster_crs <- if (!is.null(soil_stack)) crs(soil_stack) else NULL
```

The pipeline reads three local data sources. Their metadata is summarized below.

```{r inputs-table}
#| tbl-cap: "Input data summary"

input_meta <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Input, ~File, ~Format, ~Description,
  "Study-area boundary",
  basename(boundary_path),
  if (!is.null(boundary)) paste0("GeoPackage, ", nrow(boundary), " feature(s)") else "n/a",
  "Single polygon defining the crop field extent",
  "Soil-property raster",
  basename(raster_path),
  if (!is.null(soil_stack))
    paste0("GeoTIFF, ", nlyr(soil_stack), " layer(s), ",
           format(ncell(soil_stack), big.mark = ","), " cells") else "n/a",
  paste0("Multi-layer stack: ", if (!is.null(soil_stack)) paste(names(soil_stack), collapse = ", ") else "n/a"),
  "Observation points",
  basename(points_path),
  if (!is.null(points_raw)) paste0("CSV, ", nrow(points_raw), " rows, ", ncol(points_raw), " cols") else "n/a",
  paste0("Columns: ", if (!is.null(points_raw)) paste(names(points_raw), collapse = ", ") else "n/a")
)
kable(input_meta, align = "lll")
```

## 1.1 Coordinate reference system

Observation points, the soil raster, and the boundary are aligned to a common
projected CRS before extraction, crop, and mask. The boundary is reprojected to
the raster CRS (`r if (!is.null(raster_crs)) as.character(raster_crs) else "n/a"`);
point coordinates are interpreted in whichever CRS the analysis was run with
(auto-detected as geographic or projected). The raster and the boundary must
spatially overlap; otherwise the pipeline aborts with a clear error.

## 1.2 Study-area boundary

```{r boundary-fig}
#| fig-cap: "Study-area boundary"
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

if (!is.null(boundary)) {
  b <- st_make_valid(boundary)
  bb <- st_bbox(b)
  aspect <- (bb["xmax"] - bb["xmin"]) / (bb["ymax"] - bb["ymin"])
  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = b, fill = "grey85", color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
    coord_sf(crs = st_crs(b)) +
    labs(title = "Study-area boundary",
         caption = paste0("CRS: ", st_crs(b)$Name %||% st_crs(b)$input)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(axis.title = element_blank())
} else {
  ggplot() + annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "Boundary file not available") + theme_void()
}
```

# 2. Pre-processing

```{r preprocess-load}
# Align the boundary to the raster CRS so the uncertainty mask in section 7
# matches the membership rasters. An earlier version of this chunk also
# rebuilt `points_sf` and `soil_masked` to mirror the batch pipeline, but
# neither object is consumed downstream, and reconstructing points_sf
# assumed fixed coordinate column names ("longitude"/"latitude") + WGS84,
# which crash the render for any dataset with a different schema (e.g.
# projected UTM coords or differently-named columns). Both are omitted here
# so the report is robust to whatever columns the caller actually supplied.
# `raster_crs` is computed in the inputs-load chunk above.
if (!is.null(raster_crs) && !is.null(boundary) &&
    !identical(as.character(st_crs(boundary)), as.character(raster_crs))) {
  boundary <- st_transform(boundary, raster_crs)
}
```

## 2.1 Raster value extraction at observation points

Raster values were extracted at every observation point using `terra::extract` (called with the
`terra::` namespace to avoid a collision with `tidyr::extract`). Where the raster layer name
matched a column in the input CSV, the raster value overwrote the CSV value; otherwise the CSV
value was retained.

## 2.2 NA imputation and normalization

Any remaining missing values in the soil-property matrix were median-imputed per variable.
Features were then min-max normalized to the unit interval `[0, 1]` to make them commensurate
for the PCA and FCM stages.

# 3. Dimensionality reduction (PCA)

PCA was run on the normalized feature matrix (no centering or scaling, since inputs are already
in `[0, 1]`). Principal components were retained until cumulative variance reached
`r round(params$pca_threshold * 100)`%.

```{r pca}
#| fig-cap: "PCA scree plot (cumulative variance)"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

df_norm <- as.data.frame(lapply(points_raw[, SOIL_VARS, drop = FALSE], as.numeric))
df_norm <- as.data.frame(lapply(df_norm, function(x) {
  rng <- max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.finite(rng) || rng == 0) return(rep(0.5, length(x)))
  (x - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / rng
}))

pca_raw <- prcomp(df_norm, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)
eig <- pca_raw$sdev^2
prop_var <- eig / sum(eig)
cumvar <- cumsum(prop_var)
n_pc <- which(cumvar >= params$pca_threshold)[1]
if (is.na(n_pc)) n_pc <- length(eig)

pca_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  PC = seq_along(prop_var),
  Eigenvalue = round(eig, 4),
  Proportion = round(prop_var, 4),
  Cumulative = round(cumvar, 4)
)

pca_long <- pca_tbl %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Proportion, Cumulative),
               names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value")

ggplot(pca_long, aes(PC, Value, color = Metric)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = pca_tbl$PC) +
  labs(x = "Principal component", y = "Variance",
       title = "PCA variance explained",
       subtitle = paste0("Retained ", n_pc, " PCs (",
                         round(cumvar[n_pc] * 100, 1), "% cumulative)")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
```

```{r pca-table}
#| tbl-cap: "PCA eigenvalues and variance explained"

kable(pca_tbl, align = "rrrr")
```

# 4. Cluster-validity analysis

Fuzzy C-Means was fit on the retained PCA scores for every `k` in
`r paste(params$k_range, collapse = ", ")` (fuzziness m = `r params$fuzziness`,
`iter.max = 300`, fixed seed = `r params$seed`). Five cluster-validity indices were computed:

- **XB** — Xie-Beni index (lower is better)
- **FPI** — Fuzzy Performance Index (lower is better; McBratney & Moore 1985)
- **NCE** — Normalized Classification Entropy (lower is better)
- **PE** — Partition Entropy (lower is better)
- **FS** — Fukuyama-Sugeno index (lower is better)

The optimal `k` was selected as the **consensus minimum** of the XB, FPI, and NCE ranks.

```{r validation-table}
#| tbl-cap: "Cluster-validity indices and ranks"

validation_disp <- validation %>%
  mutate(across(c(XB, FPI, NCE, PE, FS), ~ signif(.x, 4))) %>%
  arrange(consensus_rank)
kable(validation_disp, align = "rrrrrrrr")
```

```{r validation-fig}
#| fig-cap: "Cluster-validity indices (min-max scaled, lower = better)"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

# Inline plot: each index min-max scaled to [0, 1] so they share a y-axis.
# Lower is better for all of them, so a "going down" trajectory is the goal.
scale01 <- function(x) {
  rng <- max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.finite(rng) || rng == 0) return(rep(0.5, length(x)))
  (x - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / rng
}

vplot_df <- rbind(
  data.frame(k = validation$k, index = "XB",  value = scale01(validation$XB)),
  data.frame(k = validation$k, index = "FPI", value = scale01(validation$FPI)),
  data.frame(k = validation$k, index = "NCE", value = scale01(validation$NCE)),
  data.frame(k = validation$k, index = "PE",  value = scale01(validation$PE)),
  data.frame(k = validation$k, index = "FS",  value = scale01(validation$FS))
)

ggplot(vplot_df, aes(k, value, color = index)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = validation$k) +
  labs(x = "Number of zones (k)",
       y = "Scaled index value (lower = better)",
       color = "Index",
       title = "Cluster-validity indices") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
```

# 5. Final FCM model and zone map

The final FCM was fit with the optimal k = `r opt_k` (consensus rank = `r min(validation$consensus_rank)`).
Each observation point was assigned to the zone with the largest membership, and the `k` membership
surfaces were interpolated across the study area by `r params$interpolation_method`.

```{r zone-fig}
#| fig-cap: "Management zone map (k = optimal)"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 7

knitr::include_graphics(file.path(OUT, "mz_zone_map.png"))
```

## 5.1 Zone assignment summary

```{r zone-assign}
#| tbl-cap: "Observation points per zone"

if (has_pa) {
  zone_counts <- point_assignments %>%
    count(Zone) %>%
    mutate(Proportion = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1))
  kable(zone_counts, align = "rrr", col.names = c("Zone", "Points", "Share (%)"))
} else {
  cat("Per-point assignments not available on disk.\n")
}
```

## 5.2 Transition mask

A cell is flagged as a transition zone when the maximum membership at that cell is below
`r params$transition_threshold`. `r if (has_transition) paste0("**", pct_transition, "%** of the study area falls in this category; the remaining ", round(100 - pct_transition, 1), "% is a \"core\" zone with a confident assignment.") else "The transition mask was not written to disk for this run (no UI control for the transition threshold was set); per-cell uncertainty is reported as Shannon entropy in section 8."`

# 6. Zone statistics

```{r zone-stats}
zone_stats <- read.csv(file.path(OUT, "mz_zone_stats.csv"))
zone_stats_disp <- zone_stats %>%
  mutate(across(-Zone, ~ signif(.x, 3)))
kable(zone_stats_disp, align = "r")
```

## 6.1 ANOVA and effect size (η²)

For each soil variable, a one-way ANOVA tested the null hypothesis that the variable mean is
equal across zones. The η² effect size is reported in addition to the p-value.

```{r anova}
#| tbl-cap: "Per-variable ANOVA and effect size"
#| message: false
#| warning: false

if (!requireNamespace("effectsize", quietly = TRUE)) {
  message("`effectsize` not available; reporting p-values only.")
  anova_disp <- read.csv(file.path(OUT, "mz_anova.csv")) %>%
    mutate(eta_sq = NA_real_) %>%
    mutate(p_value = signif(p_value, 4)) %>%
    select(variable, eta_sq, p_value)
  kable(anova_disp, align = "lrr", col.names = c("Variable", "η²", "p-value"))
} else {
  suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(effectsize))

  if (!has_pa) stop("`mz_point_assignments.csv` is required for the ANOVA section but was not found.")
  # Ensure a join key: some input CSVs omit `fid`. The pipeline assigns a
  # sequential fid (1..n) when it is missing, so replicate that here so the
  # per-point join with point_assignments stays row-aligned.
  if (!"fid" %in% names(points_raw)) points_raw$fid <- seq_len(nrow(points_raw))
  pts <- point_assignments %>% select(fid, Zone)
  df_for_aov <- points_raw %>%
    select(fid, all_of(SOIL_VARS)) %>%
    inner_join(pts, by = "fid")

  anova_disp <- do.call(rbind, lapply(SOIL_VARS, function(v) {
    fit <- aov(as.formula(paste(v, "~ factor(Zone)")), data = df_for_aov)
    p_val <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
    es <- effectsize::eta_squared(fit, partial = FALSE)
    data.frame(
      variable = v,
      eta_sq   = round(es$Eta2[1], 3),
      p_value  = signif(p_val, 4)
    )
  })) %>%
    mutate(significance = case_when(
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p_value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE            ~ ""
    ))

  kable(anova_disp, align = "lrrl",
        col.names = c("Variable", "η²", "p-value", "Sig."))
}
```

> **Note.** The η² values above are computed on the original CSV observations for transparency.
> The per-zone means in the previous table were computed on the post-extraction matrix (raster
> values overwrite CSV values where available) used during clustering. Differences are small in
> practice and only affect cells where the CSV had a value and the raster had a different one.

# 7. Uncertainty map (membership entropy)

Fuzzy assignments carry intrinsic uncertainty: a cell near the boundary between two zones will
have intermediate memberships for both. The Shannon entropy of the membership vector at each cell
summarizes this:

$$H(\mathbf{u}) = -\sum_{j=1}^{k} u_j \log u_j$$

Higher entropy ⇒ more ambiguous assignment. This is a useful diagnostic, since transition zones
defined by a hard threshold (Section 5.2) lose the gradient information.

```{r entropy}
#| fig-cap: "Membership entropy across the study area"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 7
#| message: false

memb_files <- list.files(OUT, pattern = "^mz_membership_z[0-9]+\\.tif$",
                         full.names = TRUE)

# Per-zone membership TIFs are written by the standalone pipeline and (now)
# by the Shiny app's Generate Report handler. If they are absent we skip
# the uncertainty section with a notice rather than abort the render.
has_membership <- length(memb_files) > 0L
entropy_df <- NULL
entropy_summary <- NULL

if (has_membership) {
  memb_stack <- rast(memb_files)
  memb_vals <- values(memb_stack, mat = TRUE)
  memb_vals <- pmax(memb_vals, .Machine$double.xmin)
  memb_vals <- memb_vals / rowSums(memb_vals)  # renormalize (defensive)
  entropy_vec <- -rowSums(memb_vals * log(memb_vals))

  entropy_rast <- memb_stack[[1]]
  values(entropy_rast) <- entropy_vec
  if (!is.null(boundary)) {
    entropy_rast <- mask(entropy_rast, vect(boundary))
  }
  entropy_df <- as.data.frame(entropy_rast, xy = TRUE)
  names(entropy_df) <- c("x", "y", "Entropy")

  entropy_summary <- data.frame(
    Statistic = c("Min", "1st quartile", "Median", "Mean",
                  "3rd quartile", "Max", "log(k) ceiling"),
    Value     = round(c(quantile(entropy_vec, c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.5, 0.75, 1),
                                na.rm = TRUE)[c(1, 2, 3, 5)],
                        mean(entropy_vec, na.rm = TRUE),
                        max(entropy_vec, na.rm = TRUE),
                        log(n_zones)), 4),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
} else {
  cat("Per-zone membership TIFs (mz_membership_z*.tif) are not on disk; ",
      "skipping the uncertainty map. Run delineation_management_zones.R ",
      "or click Generate Report in the Shiny app to populate them.\n")
}
```

```{r entropy-stats}
#| tbl-cap: "Entropy distribution"
if (is.null(entropy_summary)) {
  cat("No entropy data — see note in section 8.\n")
} else {
  kable(entropy_summary, align = "lr")
}
```

```{r entropy-plot}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 7

if (!is.null(entropy_df) && nrow(entropy_df) > 0) {
  ggplot(entropy_df, aes(x, y, fill = Entropy)) +
    geom_raster() +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", name = "Entropy") +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(title = "Membership entropy",
         subtitle = "Higher = more ambiguous zone assignment") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(axis.title = element_blank())
}
```

# 8. Reproducibility

```{r reproducibility}
#| results: "asis"

param_tbl <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c(
    "Fuzziness (m)",
    "k range",
    "PCA cumulative-variance threshold",
    "Transition threshold (max membership)",
    "Interpolation method",
    "Random seed"
  ),
  Value = c(
    as.character(params$fuzziness),
    paste(params$k_range, collapse = ", "),
    as.character(params$pca_threshold),
    as.character(params$transition_threshold),
    params$interpolation_method,
    as.character(params$seed)
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
knitr::asis_output(paste0(
  "<details><summary>Pipeline parameters</summary>\n\n",
  knitr::knit_print(kable(param_tbl, align = "lr", format = "html")),
  "\n</details>"
))
```

```{r session-info}
#| results: "asis"
si <- sessionInfo()
knitr::asis_output(paste0(
  "<details><summary>R session info</summary>\n\n",
  "```\n",
  paste(capture.output(print(si)), collapse = "\n"),
  "\n```\n\n</details>"
))
```

---

*Report generated by `report/mz_report.qmd` on `r format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")`.*
